brief: The dominant story remains capital flooding into AI infrastructure and models, with TeraWulf's $19 billion Anthropic lease and SambaNova's $1 billion raise at an $11 billion valuation underscoring how private markets are still writing ever-larger checks even as strategics like Tencent angle for control of promising startups. The private-public link is tightening as these bets increasingly reach the public sphere—whether through lease-backed infrastructure deals, humanoid robotics IPOs, or the leadership churn at OpenAI that signals maturing governance pressures on the sector's crown jewel. Watch whether valuations and expectations can hold as expansion accelerates broadly—Waymo's new markets among them—and capital-intensive commitments meet the reality of monetization timelines.
